title: Nickname length check uses byte count, not character count
description: ws.rs line 173: nickname.len() > 32 uses byte length. The error message says '1-32 characters'. A nickname of 9 emoji characters (each 4 bytes = 36 bytes) is rejected as too long even though it's only 9 display characters. A nickname of 32 ASCII chars is exactly 32 bytes, accepted. A nickname of 11 three-byte CJK characters (33 bytes) is rejected. The behavior differs from the documented invariant. A future contributor adding nickname validation anywhere else in the stack (CLI, mobile) could use char count and create a mismatch where the relay rejects nicknames the client considers valid. Fix: nickname.chars().count() > 32.
